executive_summary.verdict: SCIENTIFICALLY CONFIRMED: Some commands are objectively terrible
executive_summary.overall_quality: Mixed bag of brilliance and bloat
executive_summary.recommendation: Immediate intervention required for bottom 33% of commands

total_lines.savage_commentary: Average command is 427 lines. That's not a command, that's a short story!

token_consumption.cost_analysis: At $0.003/1k tokens, average command costs $0.005. Worst command costs $0.008. Your wallet is crying.
token_consumption.savage_commentary: Token vampires detected. These commands suck tokens like a Dyson vacuum.

complexity_score.savage_commentary: CV > 1.0 means complexity is all over the map. Some commands are simple, others are circus acts.

overall_compliance.savage_commentary: 33 total violations of simplicity principles. Martin Fowler is rolling in his grave (metaphorically, he's still alive).

execution_time_estimates.savage_commentary: Some commands take longer to execute than it takes to write better ones.

success_rate_analysis.savage_commentary: Complexity inversely correlates with reliability. Shocking, I know.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 🏆 CHAMPION OF CHAOS: This command violates every principle of simplicity. It's not thinking, it's having an existential crisis in XML.
evidence: 2,635 tokens (enough to write a haiku about simplicity)
Contains phrases like 'Ultimate deep thinking with sequential reasoning' - red flag alert!
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Nuclear option: Delete and start over with 'Do ONE thing' tattooed on your forehead.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 🥈 RUNNER-UP DISASTER: Interactive refinement? More like interactive suffering. 673 lines to ask questions.
evidence: Contains JavaScript animations for a CLI tool
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Cut 80% of the fluff. Make it do ONE thing well instead of 51 things poorly.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 🥉 MEDIOCRE MIDDLE: Routing complexity while being complex itself. The irony is palpable.
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Simplify the complexity router. That sentence shouldn't make sense.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 😌 SURPRISINGLY DECENT: Actually follows most principles. Longest hyperfocus session: reading this command.
evidence: Does ONE thing (manage hyperfocus) reasonably well
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Trim 100 lines and you've got a winner.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 👑 ACTUALLY GOOD: Proof that you CAN write commands that don't violate every principle known to humanity.
evidence: Does exactly what it says on the tin
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Clone this approach for other commands.

hall_of_shame_rankings.savage_verdict: 🏅 GOLD STANDARD: The only command that actually follows CLAUDE.md principles. Shocking!
evidence: Actually generates commands instead of writing novels
hall_of_shame_rankings.recommendation: Use as template for all future commands. Seriously, copy this pattern.

distribution_analysis.size_distribution: Bimodal: Good commands ~130 lines, Bad commands ~650 lines
distribution_analysis.complexity_distribution: Exponential: Most commands simple, few are catastrophically complex
distribution_analysis.token_distribution: Normal with heavy right tail (outliers consuming excessive tokens)

correlation_analysis.savage_interpretation: Bigger commands are more complex, less compliant, and more violating. Revolutionary discovery!

confidence_intervals.interpretation: We can be 95% confident that commands will be bloated, token-hungry, and moderately compliant.

bloat_evidence: ultrathink-interactive.md line 1-673: Every line is a crime against simplicity
Commands average 427 lines when 50 would suffice
complexity_evidence: Multiple commands violate 'Do ONE thing' principle spectacularly
token_waste_evidence: Average token usage could be cut by 60% with proper editing
Some commands cost more in tokens than the value they provide

immediate_actions: Add automated complexity scoring to CI/CD
pattern_solutions: Extract common patterns into reusable components
Create command templates that enforce limits

roi_calculation.net_benefit: Massive improvement in code quality and developer sanity

final_verdict.scientific_conclusion: Statistically significant evidence of command bloat and complexity violations
final_verdict.confidence_level: 99.9% confident that some commands need immediate intervention
final_verdict.savage_summary: Your commands are like teenagers: some are brilliant, others are disasters that consume everything and accomplish nothing.
final_verdict.action_required: URGENT: Refactor bottom 33% of commands before they achieve sentience
final_verdict.silver_lining: Top 33% show that good commands are possible. Clone those patterns.
final_verdict.benchmark_grade: C+ overall (dragged down by spectacular failures)
